capabilities: Combine centralized state transition management with agent planning derived knowledge base to streamline processes and ensure consistent state transitions.

procedure.action: Reconcile artifacts from both knowledge bases by applying domain-specific tooling to ensure all state transitions are captured; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail by applying field-level structural comparison on shared key fields; flag conflicts where hash(A.field) != hash(B.field) and log to conflict_log; assert merge_error_count < 1.
Assert merged output for consistency by asserting error_count < 1 and result_valid = true using domain-appropriate tooling; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Filter transition candidates by running domain-specific algorithms to identify potential state changes; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code.
Assign results using domain-specific classification tools to categorize state transitions; ensure error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code.

Log all operations to an audit trail with detailed timestamps and result codes to ensure traceability and accountability of state transitions using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Integrate centralized state transition management with agent planning derived knowledge base by merging workflows and validating outputs for consistency and accuracy.
title: Centralized State Transition Management

failureConditions: Inconsistent state transitions when merging omits critical state changes, causing downstream process failures.
Data loss when validation fails to catch errors, resulting in incomplete state transitions being logged.
Incorrect classification when domain-specific tools misinterpret state changes, leading to erroneous state management.
metrics: merged output for consistency by asserting error_count < 1 and result_valid = true using domain-appropriate tooling
